# Startup / Early-Stage Company Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-startup-startup-site
**Vertical slug:** startup
**VN-ID:** VN-PRO-001
**Niche family:** NF-PRO-TEC (standalone)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's startup ecosystem (Paystack, Flutterwave, Moove, Piggyvest alumni and emerging wave) has attracted over $2 billion in venture funding (2021 peak). Lagos is Africa's startup capital; Abuja and PH are secondary hubs. Accelerators include Y Combinator (Africa cohorts), Founders Factory Africa, FATE Foundation, CcHub, and Ventures Platform. Startups need credible websites for investor outreach, customer acquisition, and talent recruitment. CAC registration is required for legitimate fundraising.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Kemi, 35, Lagos Angel Investor**: Evaluating a seed-stage startup. Needs to see team, product demo, traction metrics, and CAC registration before scheduling a call.
- **Yusuf, 26, tech professional**: Considering joining a startup. Checks team culture, mission, and salary transparency on the site.
- **Adaeze, 40, corporate B2B buyer**: Needs to verify the startup is legitimate before procurement. Checks CAC reg, team credentials, and client list.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (mission/solution in one line), product screenshot/demo, traction metrics strip, WhatsApp demo CTA, investor logos, team teaser
- **About**: Full team profiles, mission/vision, accelerator backing, CAC details, Nigeria-first commitment
- **Services (Product)**: Product features, pricing/plans (₦), integration list, customer testimonials, FAQ
- **Contact**: WhatsApp demo request, enquiry form (NDPR-compliant), office location, social links

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Bold, mission-driven, traction-forward. "Building for Africa", "Backed by [Accelerator]", "Serving X,000 Nigerian users" language. Never overuse buzzwords without proof. Primary CTA: "Request a Demo". Secondary: "Chat on WhatsApp".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- CAC registration number
- SEC Nigeria registration (if fundraising)
- Accelerator logos (YC, FATE, CcHub)
- Traction metrics (users, GMV, growth %)
- NITDA data compliance badge
- NDPR compliance notice
- Team credentials and LinkedIn links

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Founder presenting pitch at Lagos Startup Week — packed audience, projector screen
2. Tech team at CcHub or Venture Platform co-working space — diverse, energetic
3. Product interface on laptop/phone — clean Nigerian-context UI
4. Handshake or signing ceremony — deal close, Lagos corporate setting
5. Metrics dashboard on screen — growth chart, Nigerian user count
6. Developer working late at laptop — Lagos apartment, startup hustle
7. Diverse startup team photo — branded t-shirts, startup office

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] CAC registration displayed
- [ ] SEC Nigeria reg if public investment solicitation
- [ ] NITDA NDPR compliance notice
- [ ] NDPR consent on contact/demo form
- [ ] Privacy policy linked

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp demo request CTA prominent
- [x] NGN pricing where applicable
- [x] Nigerian city/context references
- [x] Nigerian team names and profiles
- [x] CAC trust signal
- [x] Paystack payment integration mentioned
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all user inputs
- [x] NDPR consent on forms
